entities.description: standard rag models are described as systems that generate free form natural language responses
natural language responses are the output generated by standard rag models  often containing extraneous conversational text
ground truth labels are concise reference answers  e g    option a  or  12 5   used for comparison against model outputs
a 1 1 answer extraction and normalization is the title of the section discussing the process of extracting and normalizing answers
option a is an example of a concise ground truth label mentioned in the text
12 5 is an example of a concise ground truth label mentioned in the text
the answer is    is an example of extraneous conversational text that may appear in raw model outputs

relations.description: standard rag models generate natural language responses as their output
natural language responses are compared against ground truth labels  a process that can lead to false negatives if not normalized
the section a 1 1 answer extraction and normalization describes the behavior of standard rag models
the section a 1 1 answer extraction and normalization discusses the comparison with ground truth labels
option a is cited as an example of a ground truth label
12 5 is cited as an example of a ground truth label
the answer is    is cited as an example of the extraneous conversational text found in natural language responses
